Enable ISE AI chatbot file system awareness

The plugin had backend APIs for project file analysis but the system
prompt was preventing the AI from knowing it could use them. This caused
the plugin to give generic responses like 'I don't have direct access to
your file system' even when asked about the user's own project.

Changes:
- Updated STANDARD_SYSTEM_PROMPT to explicitly declare file system access
- Added examples of project analysis capabilities (file counting, searching, etc.)
- Instructed AI to use actual file analysis instead of generic responses
- Emphasized importance of referencing specific paths and file contents

Result: Now when asked 'how many files are in the tests folder?', the
chatbot will accurately analyze the tests directory and return the actual
file count instead of a generic disclaimer.

chore: improve copy_option and PR overview advice (#677)

### Motivation

GitHub Copilot reviews are making incorrect assertions about which
`copy_option` to use. Attempt to make this clearer. We also don't need
as much information in the pull request overviews, it's just duplicating
the title and automated PR description.

### Modifications

Updated copilot-instructions.md guidance.

### Verification

commit.message:
Fix recursive HTML nesting bug in JetBrains plugin streaming

The plugin was displaying corrupted nested HTML tags because the
streamAssistantChunk() function attempted to extract plain text from
rendered HTML and re-wrap it for each chunk, causing progressive
HTML tag nesting.

Solution: Maintain a separate plain text buffer (currentMessageText)
to track accumulated response text. Each chunk is appended to this
buffer and the entire accumulated text is formatted once.

Changes:
- Added currentMessageText field to track accumulated response
- Simplified chunk handling to append to buffer then reformat
- Reset buffer on message completion

This fixes display issues with both simple queries and web search
responses in the plugin interface.

commit.message:
security: add runtime check to prevent RUNNER_TEMP=/tmp collision with runtime tree (#25176)

* Initial plan

* security: add runtime check to prevent RUNNER_TEMP=/tmp collision with runtime tree

When RUNNER_TEMP resolves to /tmp on custom/self-hosted runners, the setup
tree (${RUNNER_TEMP}/gh-aw, mounted read-only) and the runtime tree
(/tmp/gh-aw, mounted read-write) collapse into the same directory. This
would allow the agent to overwrite compiled scripts, prompts, and MCP configs
that must remain immutable.

Add a guard in actions/setup/setup.sh that resolves RUNNER_TEMP to its
canonical path and fails fast if it equals /tmp.

fix: exempt gh-aw internal secrets from safe update enforcement (#25143)

Secrets like GH_AW_GITHUB_TOKEN, GH_AW_GITHUB_MCP_SERVER_TOKEN, and
COPILOT_GITHUB_TOKEN are automatically injected by the compiler based on
frontmatter tool configuration, not by user/AI-authored content.

Previously, these were flagged as "new unapproved secrets" on first
compilation in strict mode (since strict mode implies safe update
enforcement), causing TestWorkflowRunBranchValidation to fail.

Add a ghAwInternalSecrets allowlist alongside GITHUB_TOKEN so that
compiler-managed secrets are never treated as safe-update violations.

commit.message:
fix: work around DevFlow.Blazor packaging bug breaking Windows build (#532)

## Problem

The `Microsoft.Maui.DevFlow.Blazor` NuGet package
(0.1.0-preview.4.26202.3) has a packaging bug: its PRI file references
wwwroot assets under `lib/` but the package only ships them under
`staticwebassets/`. This causes **MSB3030** errors on Windows:

```
error MSB3030: Could not copy the file "...\Microsoft.Maui.DevFlow.Blazor\wwwroot\chobitsu.js" because it was not found.
```

This has been **breaking every Build & Release CI run** since the
DevFlow.Blazor package was added — the Windows build fails, which blocks
`create-release`, resulting in **v1.0.18 shipping with zero GitHub
Release artifacts**.

## Fix

Add a MSBuild target (`_FixDevFlowBlazorWwwroot`) that copies the two
missing files from `staticwebassets/` to the expected `lib/.../wwwroot/`
location. Key safety features:

- **Windows-only** — condition checks for `windows` target platform
(Mac/Android/iOS unaffected)
- **Source-existence guards** —
`Exists('$(_DevFlowStaticSrc)\chobitsu.js')` prevents build errors if
the package version changes
- **Idempotent** — `SkipUnchangedFiles` + destination-existence checks

## Verification

- ✅ Mac Catalyst build succeeds (0 errors)
- ✅ All 3300 tests pass
- ✅ The CI error message matches exactly what this target fixes

## After merge

Consider re-tagging v1.0.18 or creating v1.0.19 to get artifacts
published.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
